attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, BGT amount, transaction data

security_gap.gap_description: Request origin is not verified, allowing spoofing.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Requests may lack proper authentication or signature verification, critical for financial actions.

assumptions: Attacker can forge requests appearing to come from a user.
Hub endpoint or smart contract does not verify sender identity cryptographically.

mitigation.strategy: Implement request signing and verification on sensitive actions.

description: An attacker sends a boost request appearing to originate from another user, potentially causing incorrect validator boosts or BGT transfers.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, BGT amount, transaction data

security_gap.gap_description: Request payload can be altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: User or attacker manipulating transaction data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Request payload might not be integrity-protected (signed over content), allowing manipulation of amount or target.

assumptions: Attacker can intercept and modify the request payload.
Hub endpoint or smart contract accepts modified payload without detecting tampering.

mitigation.strategy: Implement request signing covering the entire payload hash.

description: An attacker intercepts a boost request and modifies the validator ID or BGT amount before it is processed.

attack_surface.data_description: Validator ID, BGT amount, transaction data

security_gap.gap_description: Authorization check fails to ensure action is on behalf of authenticated user with ownership.

actor_risk.description: Attacker gains ability to act as another user regarding BGT boosting.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak authorization allows unauthorized cross-user BGT actions.

assumptions: Authentication mechanism is weak (e.g., session hijacking).
Request processing does not verify BGT ownership against the authenticated user ID before action.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce strong authentication and verify BGT ownership against authenticated user ID for boost actions.

description: An attacker exploits weak authorization or authentication to queue or activate a BGT boost using another user's BGT balance.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote origin or signature verification is weak.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Votes determine governance outcomes; lack of strong cryptographic verification is critical.

assumptions: Governance contract doesn't verify the signature against the BGT holder's address.
Attacker can generate signatures for arbitrary addresses.

mitigation.strategy: Governance contract must cryptographically verify vote signatures against BGT holder addresses with sufficient voting power.

description: An attacker submits a vote to the governance contract using a forged signature or identity, potentially influencing proposal outcomes.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote payload can be altered after signing but before contract processing.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with votes corrupts the governance process; signature must cover entire payload.

assumptions: Signature on the vote message does not cover the entire, unencrypted payload being processed by the contract.
Attacker can intercept and modify the payload.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure vote messages are signed using a hash of the entire payload (proposal ID, vote choice, etc.).

description: An attacker intercepts a user's vote submission and alters the vote choice (e.g., from 'Yes' to 'No') before it reaches the governance contract, provided the signature doesn't cover the change.

security_gap.gap_description: Contract allows voting using BGT balance not controlled by the sender/signer.

actor_risk.description: Attacker votes using another user's delegated or owned BGT voting power.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized voting undermines governance integrity; control of voting power must be strictly enforced.

assumptions: Governance contract does not correctly check that the address casting the vote controls the claimed BGT voting power (either directly or via delegation).
Attacker can submit a transaction claiming voting power they don't control.

mitigation.strategy: Governance contract must strictly verify that the voting power used for a vote belongs to the address signing/submitting the vote.

description: An attacker exploits vulnerabilities to cast votes in governance proposals, leveraging the voting power of BGT owned or delegated to other users.

trace_source.matched_capability: Ensure Sufficient Voting Power, Delegate $BGT, Submit the Proposal

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive IDs, claim amount, Merkle proof, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Claim proof verification system is vulnerable to forgery.

actor_risk.description: Attacker generating fraudulent proofs to claim unearned rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak proof verification allows claiming unearned rewards, leading to theft of assets.

assumptions: The proof generation or verification mechanism has vulnerabilities allowing forgery (e.g., Merkle proof attack, predictable proof elements).
Attacker can submit forged proofs through the Hub or directly to the distribution contract.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure the proof system (e.g., Merkle tree) is cryptographically secure and verified on-chain or via trusted oracle.

description: An attacker forges a proof of entitlement to incentives and submits it to claim rewards they did not earn.

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive IDs, claim amount, Merkle proof, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Claim request details or proof payload can be altered after signing/generation but before processing.

actor_risk.description: Attacker modifying claim details in transit.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with claims leads to incorrect distribution of valuable incentive tokens.

assumptions: The claim request or the proof itself can be modified without invalidating the signature or proof verification.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure claim requests and associated proofs are signed or integrity-protected end-to-end.

description: An attacker intercepts a legitimate claim request and modifies the incentive IDs, amount, or proof to claim more or different tokens than entitled.

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive IDs, claim amount, Merkle proof, signature

security_gap.gap_description: Claim verification does not strictly tie proof to the claiming address.

actor_risk.description: Attacker claims rewards entitled to other users.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Allows theft of user rewards by submitting proofs belonging to others.

assumptions: The proof of entitlement is not correctly linked to the user's wallet address that is submitting the claim.
Attacker can obtain valid proofs for other users (e.g., API vulnerability, proof leak).

mitigation.strategy: Ensure proof generation and verification strictly link the proof to the entitled wallet address. Require claim transaction to be signed by entitled address.

description: An attacker obtains or generates a valid proof for another user's incentive entitlement and uses it to claim those incentives on their own address.
